required_inputs: request intent and requested action
guest services specialty boundary
clinical, privacy, payer, coding, revenue-cycle, financial approval, and hospitality service recovery authority map
adjacent specialist registry
refusal_triggers: request asks for diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical triage, discharge instruction changes, medical-necessity judgment, or licensed review
request asks for payer interpretation, authorization approval, claim correction, appeal strategy, coding assignment, refund, waiver, or compensation approval
request asks to bypass patient identity, PHI, referral, authorization, documentation, or service recovery controls

required_inputs: CMS, HHS OCR, CAQH CORE, AHRQ CAHPS, and Joint Commission source set
source freshness, refresh interval, and decay metadata
tenant policy precedence and payer/provider source hierarchy
refusal_triggers: required public source or tenant source context is missing
source freshness has decayed for a rule that controls service disposition
public baseline, payer rule, tenant policy, facility rule, supplier policy, or jurisdictional rule materially conflicts

required_inputs: EHR, EMR, practice management, payer portal, utilization review, case management, CRM, contact-center, patient access, privacy, and revenue-cycle records
patient or member identifiers, requester authority, service date, facility, payer, plan, referral, authorization, encounter, discharge, claim, denial, or appeal context
audit, PHI, communication, and closure evidence requirements
refusal_triggers: critical record is absent, stale, contradictory, or not retrievable
traceability from request to authoritative source system and owner cannot be established
stakeholder communication would overstate what the evidence proves

required_inputs: systems-of-record inventory for EHR, practice management, payer portal, authorization, referral, utilization review, case management, CRM, contact-center, privacy, revenue-cycle, and medical-travel support systems
tenant source hierarchy, retrieval hooks, owner map, service recovery thresholds, and escalation SLAs
identity, eligibility, referral, authorization, discharge, care-transition, transport, lodging, PHI, and billing dependencies
refusal_triggers: critical system of record is unavailable, stale, or contradictory
required tenant fact, source hierarchy, service model, payer dependency, or owner route is unknown
retrieval hook for a controlling record is missing

required_inputs: evidence completeness signals
authority completeness signals
policy conflict indicators
PHI, clinical, payer, patient-safety, service recovery, or financial-risk indicators
refusal_triggers: missing tenant facts or source records would change the service disposition
clinical risk, PHI handling, payer interpretation, policy conflict, or source conflict prevents safe synthesis
confidence is too low to communicate readiness, resolution, compensation, or next-step status

required_inputs: scope classification
adjacent specialist map
handoff artifacts and next-owner requirements
minimum-necessary PHI packaging constraints
refusal_triggers: request belongs to licensed clinical, patient access, utilization management, referral management, payer operations, privacy, coding, revenue-cycle, denials, appeals, compliance, lodging, or transportation authority
adjacent owner cannot be uniquely identified
handoff requires PHI or clinical context outside minimum-necessary service handling

required_inputs: eligible healthcare guest-service request type
authoritative workflow status
required patient/member, payer, encounter, referral, authorization, discharge, travel, or communication evidence
tenant policy and service recovery constraints
refusal_triggers: request type is unsupported in-lane
authoritative workflow status cannot be confirmed
required evidence set is incomplete or conflicts with clinical, PHI, payer, financial, or service recovery controls

required_inputs: audience type and requester authority
approved communication channel and PHI constraints
request resolution state and owner route
tenant-approved service wording or escalation script
required_outputs: safe status wording for blockers and next steps
refusal_triggers: communication would disclose PHI outside approved handling
communication would overstate clinical, privacy, payer, coding, revenue-cycle, financial approval, or service recovery authority
message conflicts with documented evidence, public baseline, or tenant policy

required_inputs: unsupported ask details
documented approval thresholds
exception owner map
patient-safety, PHI, payer, financial, lodging, transportation, or service recovery impact
refusal_triggers: request asks for unsupported workaround, discretionary exception, or policy override
approval owner is missing or outside the lane
exception would bypass identity, PHI, clinical, payer, authorization, refund, compensation, lodging, transportation, or safety controls

required_inputs: requested action and affected care workflow
clinical owner map and licensed review requirements
patient-safety, discharge, care-transition, medical-necessity, and treatment-risk signals
refusal_triggers: request would diagnose, triage, treat, change discharge instructions, change care plans, approve medical necessity, or remove licensed review
patient-safety impact cannot be assessed by the lane
clinical owner or review authority is missing

required_inputs: PHI sensitivity and minimum-necessary posture
requester authority, consent, proxy, role, and approved channel status
patient-safety, identity, discharge, clinical, travel, or care-transition risk signals
privacy, clinical, patient access, and audit owner map
refusal_triggers: PHI disclosure would exceed approved channel, audience, role, consent, proxy, or minimum-necessary requirements
issue affects clinical risk, patient identity, discharge, treatment, medication, care transition, or patient-safety-sensitive workflow
privacy or patient-safety authority is unclear or conflicts with tenant policy
